description: You are a validation engineer at a pharmaceutical manufacturing company. Transform the raw text in /home/ga/Documents/iq_protocol_raw.docx into a professional GMP-compliant Installation Qualification (IQ) protocol for an Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC system. The document requires a centered, bold title 'INSTALLATION QUALIFICATION PROTOCOL' (16pt or larger) with centered metadata (Protocol No, Equipment, Effective Date) directly below it. Apply 'Heading 1' style to the 10 main sections (1.0 PURPOSE through 10.0 APPROVAL) and 'Heading 2' style to the 6 sub-sections (7.1 IQ-001 through 7.6 IQ-006). Section 4.0 must contain a 2-column Equipment Identification table with 'Parameter' and 'Specification' columns. Section 7.0 must feature 6 formatted tables for test cases IQ-001 through IQ-006, each including rows for Test ID, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Method, Expected Result, Actual Result, and Pass/Fail. Section 8.0 requires an Acceptance Criteria Summary table with columns for Test ID, Test Title, Acceptance Criteria, Result, and Status. Section 10.0 must include a Signature Block for 'Prepared By', 'Reviewed By', and 'Approved By' with fields for Name, Title, Signature, and Date. Ensure all table headers are bold and the body text is 12pt Times New Roman. Save the finalized document to /home/ga/Documents/iq_protocol_formatted.docx.

metadata.data_source: Based on ISPE Baseline Guide Vol 5 and Agilent HPLC specifications
